contest.title: Neowise Labs Contest 1 (Codeforces Round 1018, Div. 1 + Div. 2)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: answer whether a contiguous range of interval statements can all be simultaneously true; total n, m, q <= 2e5.
Official tutorial source for the round; used to confirm the monotone satisfiable-window approach and the required interval data structure direction.
Chinese writeup explicitly describes using two pointers, range-add/range-min segment tree, and endpoint-indexed multisets to detect when a type-1 interval becomes fully covered.
Independent implementation-oriented writeup cross-checks the segment-tree operations for finding uncovered boundaries and maintaining type-1 intervals.

annotation.summary: Given interval statements saying either every viewer in [l,r] is a crewmate or at least one viewer in [l,r] is an impostor, answer whether each queried contiguous block of statements is jointly satisfiable.
annotation.constraints: The sums of n, m, and q over test cases are each <= 2e5, so each statement can only enter and leave the maintained window O(1) times with logarithmic data-structure work.
annotation.core_idea: For each right endpoint r, compute the minimum left endpoint mn[r] such that statements mn[r]..r are satisfiable. Satisfiability is monotone under deleting statements, so mn[r] can be maintained with a sliding window over statement indices. In a current window, type-0 statements add coverage over viewer positions forced to be crewmates. A type-1 statement is contradictory exactly when its whole interval has positive coverage, leaving no possible impostor. Maintain coverage counts with a lazy segment tree supporting range add and range minimum / nearest-zero queries, and maintain active type-1 intervals by endpoint so that after adding or removing a type-0 interval the algorithm can detect whether any type-1 interval lies inside the newly fully covered component. Then query [l,r] is YES iff l >= mn[r].
annotation.complexity: O((n + m + q) log n) amortized per test file, with logarithmic factors from segment tree and ordered sets
tricks: Use monotonicity of satisfiable statement windows to reduce range queries to mn[r].
Convert type-0 statements into coverage counts over positions.
A type-1 statement fails exactly when its interval minimum coverage is positive.
When a new type-0 interval merges covered components, check endpoint-indexed active type-1 intervals contained in the covered component.

solution_variants.name: sliding-window-covered-components
solution_variants.summary: Sweep the right endpoint over statements. Insert the new statement into the active window; while a contradiction exists, delete the leftmost statement and advance the left endpoint. The data structure maintains type-0 coverage over viewer positions and active type-1 intervals, so contradictions can be detected after each update without scanning all statements.

solution_variants.name: range-min-validation-view
solution_variants.summary: For a fixed set of statements, add +1 to every type-0 interval. A type-1 interval is valid iff the minimum coverage on its range is zero. This simple characterization explains the segment-tree state, but applying it independently to every query would be too slow.
solution_variants.complexity: O((m + q) log n) per independent window if used naively

tags.evidence: The main algorithm computes the minimal satisfiable left endpoint for every right endpoint by moving each statement into and out of one sliding window.
The core decision problem is whether interval constraints of type all-crewmate and at-least-one-impostor can be simultaneously satisfied.
The active window needs range add for type-0 intervals and range minimum / nearest uncovered-position queries to test whether type-1 intervals are fully covered.
tags.description: A lazy segment tree pattern where intervals receive additive updates and queries ask for the minimum value on a range or positions/components attaining the minimum.
tags.created_reason: The existing segment-tree tag is too broad for problems whose decisive data structure is range-add plus minimum/zero-component maintenance.

tags.evidence: Range coverage updates must be pushed lazily to keep each type-0 insertion/deletion logarithmic.
One standard implementation keeps active type-1 intervals in endpoint-indexed sets/multisets to find whether a fully covered component contains such an interval.
If a statement window is satisfiable, deleting statements preserves satisfiability; this monotonicity justifies the mn[r] threshold.
